hypothesis: IF the `helen_say` capability is a gap, THEN there exists no live shell symbol that dispatches to helen_talk with --reply --no-receipt. Observable signal: `type helen_say` in an interactive zsh returns 'not found'.

experiment.action: Resolve `helen_say` as a shell symbol in an interactive (login-init) zsh; cross-check the backing runner `_helen_run`; cross-check sibling helpers `helen_hal` and `helen_dev`.
experiment.system_layer: user-env (~/.zshrc) — outside repo, non-sovereign to HELEN kernel

metric.name: capability_gap_present
metric.baseline: unknown (carried over from tranche list as candidate gap)
metric.observed: absent — helen_say is a shell function at ~/.zshrc:46 dispatching `_helen_run --reply --no-receipt "$@"`

evidence.runner_definition_line: ~/.zshrc:25 — `_helen_run()` resolves ledger from HELEN_LEDGER_MODE (memory|dev|sovereign) then dispatches to `helen talk`
evidence.smoke_test: zsh -i -c 'type helen_say _helen_run helen_hal helen_dev' → all four reported as 'shell function from /Users/jean-marietassy/.zshrc'

regression_check.rationale: Zero files touched. The experiment is purely a read against the user shell environment.

close_conditions: helen_say resolves as live shell function

note: E16 was carried on the tranche list as a candidate capability gap. Direct measurement shows the gap is absent: `helen_say`, `helen_hal`, `helen_dev`, and `_helen_run` are all live zsh functions installed at ~/.zshrc. This matches the persisted note in MEMORY.md ('Working chat command: helen talk --reply --no-receipt --ledger :memory:') and the shell-function block committed to ~/.zshrc under `# HELEN OS — unified entrypoint`. No action is taken on the user shell. If a future user reports `command not found`, the first diagnostic is `source ~/.zshrc` or `echo $ZDOTDIR`; no HELEN-kernel change is implied.
